items.title: Could Investing $10,000 in Bloom Energy Make You a Millionaire?
items.summary: Bloom Energy has surged over 1,100% in the past year, driven by AI infrastructure demand and major partnerships. However, a $10,000 investment today would need a 100x gain to reach $1 million, requiring Bloom's market cap to reach $8-9 trillion. While demand is real, the author cautions that the path from demand to revenue is complex due to financing structures and project timelines, requiring near-perfect execution to sustain momentum.

items.title: 2 Nvidia-Owned Stocks Investors Should Buy Now
items.summary: Nvidia's investment portfolio includes CoreWeave and Nokia, two companies positioned to benefit from AI growth. CoreWeave, a neocloud provider, has a $99 billion backlog and 112% revenue growth but carries significant debt concerns. Nokia, pivoting to AI-enabled telecom equipment through its Nvidia partnership, shows modest revenue growth but has surged 170% over the past year, with potential upside as 5G and 6G deployments accelerate.

items.title: Nebius Stock's Massive Upside Comes With Serious Risks
items.summary: Nebius has emerged as an intriguing AI infrastructure stock with strong revenue growth and hyperscaler agreements, but faces significant challenges including valuation risk, capital intensity, and execution pressure. The key question is whether the company can become foundational AI infrastructure and justify the enormous expectations already priced into its stock.

items.title: Forget the "Magnificent Seven": These 3 Hypergrowth Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks Are Just Getting Started
items.summary: Three AI-focused stocks—Micron, Sandisk, and Nebius—are experiencing hypergrowth rates far exceeding the Magnificent Seven. Micron and Sandisk are capitalizing on a memory chip shortage driven by AI infrastructure demand, with projected growth of 197% and 167% respectively in fiscal 2026. Nebius, a neocloud company building AI-focused data centers, is growing even faster with 684% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 and expectations of 20x revenue growth by 2027.

items.title: 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Infrastructure Stocks Outgrowing Nvidia
items.summary: Nebius and CoreWeave, two neocloud companies backed by Nvidia, are growing faster than Nvidia itself. Nebius posted 684% Q1 revenue growth with projections reaching $11.2 billion by 2027, while CoreWeave achieved 112% Q1 growth with a $100 billion order backlog. However, both companies face risks due to their reliance on capital raises that could dilute shareholders.

items.title: Why Nebius Stock Soared to a New All-Time High Today
items.summary: Nebius Group stock climbed to a record high after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang praised the neocloud company as a 'world-class AI cloud' during COMPUTEX. Huang highlighted Nebius's rapid growth and notable customers including Cursor, World Labs, Revolut, and Shopify. The surge also follows news that Leopold Aschenbrenner's Situational Awareness hedge fund acquired over 12.4 million Nebius shares valued at $3.3 billion.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq Futures Fall After NVDA's Q1 Results— Nebius Group, Intuit In Focus (UPDATED)
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed mixed performance on Thursday following Nvidia's strong Q1 earnings and positive Q2 guidance. While the Dow Jones and S&P 500 gained slightly, the Nasdaq fell modestly. Intuit tumbled 13.5% despite beating earnings due to announced workforce cuts, while e.l.f. Beauty surged 9% on better-than-expected results. Nebius Group and Bloom Energy rose after announcing a 10-year partnership for AI infrastructure deployment.

items.title: NVIDIA-Backed Nebius Partners With Bloom Energy For AI Cloud Expansion
items.summary: Nebius Group announced a partnership with Bloom Energy to expand its AI cloud infrastructure using fuel cell technology for clean energy solutions. The company reported explosive Q1 2026 growth with 684% revenue increase to $399 million and raised its contracted power guidance to 4 gigawatts by year-end. The first joint project is expected to be operational this year with 328 MW capacity. NBIS shares surged 8.40% in premarket trading.

items.title: Why Nebius Stock Jumped Today
items.summary: Nebius Group stock surged 15.88% after the AI cloud infrastructure provider raised its growth forecast, citing unprecedented demand for high-performance computing. The company is expanding into inference and agentic AI workloads through acquisitions, improving profitability with EBITDA reaching $129.5 million, and targeting over 4 gigawatts of power capacity by end of 2026 with plans for $7-9 billion in annual recurring revenue.
